# DLinRT.eu — Deep Learning in Radiotherapy

> A peer-reviewed catalogue of AI and deep-learning products used in radiotherapy. Independent reference for clinicians, researchers, and vendors covering auto-contouring, treatment planning, image synthesis, registration, reconstruction, performance monitoring, and more.

DLinRT.eu tracks commercially available and pipeline AI/DL products for radiotherapy with structured metadata: regulatory status (CE/FDA/TGA/TFDA), evidence rigor (E0–E3) and clinical impact (I0–I5), training/evaluation data transparency, supported anatomies and structures, modality, and manufacturer information. Reviews follow a published rubric; companies can certify their listings.

## Pages

- [Home](/): Overview and entry point to the catalogue.
- [Products](/products): Browse all commercially available AI/DL radiotherapy products.
- [Pipeline products](/products/pipeline): Pre-market and pipeline AI/DL products.
- [Companies](/companies): Vendors and manufacturers offering AI/DL solutions.
- [Initiatives](/initiatives): Grand challenges, open datasets, and model zoos.
- [Compare structures](/compare/structures): Matrix comparison of supported anatomical structures across vendors.
- [News](/news): Updates on products, regulations, and the field.
- [Dashboard](/dashboard): Aggregate analytics and visualisations of the catalogue.
- [Timeline](/timeline): Historical evolution of products and approvals.
- [Changelog](/changelog): Recent additions and changes to the catalogue.
- [Transparency](/transparency): Methodology and editorial transparency.
- [Resources & Compliance](/resources-compliance): Guidelines, references, and compliance resources.
- [Evidence & Impact guide](/evidence-impact-guide): Methodology for evidence rigor and clinical impact classification.
- [About](/about): Mission, team, and governance.
- [Support](/support): Donate or support the project.
- [Roles](/roles): Reviewer, company, and admin role overview.
- [Roles FAQ](/roles/faq): Frequently asked questions about contributor roles.

## Optional

- [Privacy policy](/privacy-policy): GDPR data handling.
- [Terms of use](/terms-of-use): Usage terms.
- [Security](/security): Security policy and reporting.
